articles.title: AI Deepfakes Bill NO FAKES Reintroduced in Congress Again
articles.description: AI deepfakes bill the NO FAKES Act has been reintroduced in Congress again — this time with additional support from Spotify and Getty.
articles.content: An updated version of the Nurture Originals, Foster Art, and Keep Entertainment Safe (NO FAKES) Act was reintroduced in the U.S. House of Representatives and Senate on Wednesday (May 20) with a wide coalition of music industry support, including from... [3524 chars]

articles.title: Research shows educational institutes must not put too much faith in AI text detectors
articles.description: UF researchers tested the five most popular AI text detectors and found false negative rates as high as 99.6%. A single vocabulary tweak defeated most of them entirely.
articles.content:
Here’s an uncomfortable thought for every academic institution currently using AI detectors to police student and researcher submissions: the tools don’t work as reliably as institutions assume.

articles.title: Was a Story That Just Won a Literary Prize A.I.-Generated?
articles.description: A respected literary magazine has published an award-winning short story many readers believe to be generated by artificial intelligence. Experts aren’t all so sure.
articles.content:
Concerns over the increasingly widespread use of A.I. tools to assist with writing continue to roil the literary world.

articles.title: The biggest revelations from SpaceX's S-1 filing
articles.description: SpaceX filed its S-1 on Wednesday, giving the public its first look at the inner workings of Elon Musk's rocket company and AI venture xAI.
articles.content:

articles.title: Zuckerberg says he feels ‘weight’ of Meta layoffs
articles.description: Mark Zuckerberg addresses Meta layoffs, expressing gratitude to departing employees while emphasizing the company's AI-focused future.
articles.content:
Meta began laying off roughly 8,000 employees on Wednesday (May 20, 2026) — about 10% of its global workforce — as co-founder and Chief Executive Mark Zuckerberg pushes to redirect resources toward an ambitious artificial intelligence agenda.

articles.title: Jim Cramer says the world of tech investing has changed and it's not going back
articles.description: CNBC's Jim Cramer said semiconductor and AI infrastructure stocks have replaced software as the market's technology leaders.
articles.content:
CNBC's Jim Cramer said that semiconductor stocks have become the market's new center of gravity because they are the drivers of the artificial intelligence boom.

articles.title: US government is using AI more, but hiring gaps and risk culture still slow progress
articles.description: As is every large organization, the U.S. government is assessing how to best integrate artificial intelligence into its procedures and workflows. While AI has undeniable risks, it also has the potential to make work significantly more efficient and effective in a broad range of ways, from automating simpler tasks to unearthing unexpected insights.

articles.title: Pope Leo to release first encyclical, addressing AI and human dignity
articles.description: Pope Leo XIV will release the first encyclical of his papacy next week, the Vatican announced Wednesday.
articles.content:
May 20 (UPI) -- Pope Leo XIV will release the first encyclical of his papacy next week, the Vatican announced Wednesday. Magnifica Humanitas will address artificial intelligence and human dignity.
